_meta.description: Steel AeroBlade Double Deflection Supply Grille, long front blades, 3/4in blade spacing, with opposed blade damper (per GRD schedule note 1)
_meta.nc_correction_applied: +7 NC added to every raw table value, per cutsheet note: 'For deflection settings of 22.5 or 45, increase stated sound levels by 1 or 7 NC respectively' (base table is referenced to 0 deg deflection)
_meta.model_correction_applied: 272 with damper = +0 NC, 1.00x pressure, 1.00x throw (no further adjustment needed) per Correction Factors for AeroBlade Supply Grilles table, page G22
_meta.target_nc: NC < 20 (raw table NC must be < 13 to survive the +7 correction)
_meta.size_ceiling_decision: Capped at the '24x24'/'48x12/36x16' nominal size tier (core area 3.75-4.00 sq ft, max 1500 cfm) per user 2026-07-17
_meta.ARCHITECTURE_CHANGE_2026-07-17: Superseded the earlier Min/Max-Flow-per-Type + 51-Types + Type-Catalog approach. Duct Width and Duct Height are now INSTANCE parameters (converted from Type via FamilyManager.MakeInstance), driven by a 32-step nested IF formula keyed on Flow. The family now has just 2 Types: 'Regular' (closest-to-square pick per CFM breakpoint) and 'Thin' (thinnest/smallest-height pick per breakpoint, for locations where a shallow duct/soffit forces an elongated shape) - Type Selector itself is the thin-vs-square toggle, via a Type-level Yes/No parameter 'Thin Profile' that the Width/Height formulas branch on. This means you literally just place the diffuser, pick Regular or Thin, type in Flow, and the actual geometry auto-sizes - true one-shot auto-sizing, matching how the ceiling diffuser family works.
_meta.min_flow_note: Per-size Min Flow (2-steps-down logic) is now MOOT since there's no longer a discrete 'is this size right for this flow' Type-selection question - the formula always produces the smallest suitable size automatically. Min Flow=0/Max Flow=1500 on both Types now just represents the family's overall valid range, used only by the Sizing Warning check (Flow > 1500 = over the family's absolute capacity, regardless of Regular/Thin).
_meta.closest_to_square_vs_thinnest_logic: For each of the 32 CFM breakpoints, if the underlying Titus cutsheet group has multiple W x H alternates at the same performance, 'Regular' picks whichever alternate has aspect ratio closest to 1:1 (subject to the 2:1 cap below), 'Thin' picks whichever has the largest aspect ratio without exceeding 4:1. Single-alternate breakpoints (e.g. 6x6, 8x8, 10x10 which are already square) are identical between Regular and Thin.
_meta.removed_types_reason: These existing family Types (from before this session's rebuild) did not correspond to any Titus 272RL published nominal size - removed 2026-07-17 rather than fabricate performance data
_meta.BUGFIX_2026-07-24: 4 of 32 breakpoints below were WRONG until this date (12x10/20x6: 432->360, 22x6: 462->385, 12x12/24x6: 528->440, 18x10/30x6: 666->555). Root cause: original build picked the 600fpm column at each of these 4 rows when 500fpm was actually the last column satisfying raw NC<13 (600fpm's raw NC was 16-18 at these 4 rows, failing the <13 requirement). Found via precise page.get_text('words') coordinate re-extraction, not visual re-read. Fixed live in both the Supply Diffuser - Sidewall (CFM Auto-Sizing) and Return Grate - Sidewall (CFM Auto-Sizing) families same day. Re-sorting the full 32-entry array was required (not just swapping 4 numbers) since the corrected values changed their ascending-order position relative to neighbors. This JSON now reflects the corrected/re-sorted values.
_meta.RATIO_CAPS_2026-07-24: User asked for a max aspect ratio of 4:1 on Thin and 2:1 on Regular (originally requested 1.5:1 for Regular, revised to 2:1 after review). Regular picks were UNCHANGED by this -- every tier's Regular was already the closest-to-square available option, so tightening the cap only changed which tiers are flagged compliant vs. an unavoidable violation (see below), not which size gets used. Thin picks changed at 6 tiers where the old most-elongated pick exceeded 4:1: 540 (36x6 6:1 excluded -> 27x8 3.375:1, a genuine 3-way group with a real middle option, NOT a collapse to Regular), 555 (30x6 5:1 -> 18x10 1.8:1, collapses to Regular, only 2 group members), 636 (42x6 7:1 -> 18x14 1.29:1, collapses, only 2 members), 728 (48x6 8:1 AND 36x8 4.5:1 both excluded -> 24x12 2:1, a genuine 4-way group with a real middle option, NOT a collapse), 856 (42x8 5.25:1 -> 24x14 1.71:1, collapses, only 2 members), 984 (48x8 6:1 -> 24x16 1.5:1, collapses, only 2 members), 1244 (48x10 4.8:1 -> 24x20 1.2:1, collapses, only 2 members). NOT YET APPLIED to the live Revit family as of this JSON update -- pending the user switching Family Editor focus to the Supply Sidewall family (was mid-session on the Return family when this documentation was written); apply the same fix here once that happens.
_meta.regular_2to1_cap_unavoidable_violations: 7 tiers exceed the 2:1 Regular cap with no better real alternative available (single-published-size tiers, or every group member exceeds 2:1): 14x6 (2.33:1), 18x6 (3:1), 22x6 (3.67:1), 22x10 (2.2:1), 24x10 (2.4:1, other option 30x8 is worse at 3.75:1), 30x12 (2.5:1, other option 36x10 is worse at 3.6:1), 36x14 (2.57:1, other option 42x12 is worse at 3.5:1). These are real Titus catalog limits, not something a different size selection could fix.
